lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਕਾਮਨਾ ਤੀਰਥ ਜਾਇ ਬਸਿਓ ਸਿਰਿ ਕਰਵਤ ਧਰਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: man kaamanaa teerath jaae basio sir karavat dharaae |
lines.english: His mind's desires may lead him to go and dwell at sacred places of pilgrimage, and offer his head to be sawn off;

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਨਿਕ ਕਾਮਿਨੀ ਹੈਵਰ ਗੈਵਰ ਬਹੁ ਬਿਧਿ ਦਾਨੁ ਦਾਤਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: kanik kaaminee haivar gaivar bahu bidh daan daataaraa |
lines.english: He may give gifts of all sorts - gold, women, horses and elephants.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਕੀ ਮੈਲੁ ਨ ਉਤਰੈ ਇਹ ਬਿਧਿ ਜੇ ਲਖ ਜਤਨ ਕਰਾਏ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: man kee mail na utarai ih bidh je lakh jatan karaae |3|
lines.english: but this will not cause the filth of his mind to depart, even though he may make thousands of efforts. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਜਾ ਅਰਚਾ ਬੰਦਨ ਡੰਡਉਤ ਖਟੁ ਕਰਮਾ ਰਤੁ ਰਹਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: poojaa arachaa bandan ddanddaut khatt karamaa rat rahataa |
lines.english: He may remain devoted to worship and adoration, bowing his forehead to the floor, practicing the six religious rituals.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਨ ਬਸਤ੍ਰ ਭੂਮਿ ਬਹੁ ਅਰਪੇ ਨਹ ਮਿਲੀਐ ਹਰਿ ਦੁਆਰਾ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: an basatr bhoom bahu arape neh mileeai har duaaraa |4|
lines.english: He may make offerings of corn, clothes and land in abundance, but this will not lead him to the Lord's Door. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਹਉ ਕਰਤ ਬੰਧਨ ਮਹਿ ਪਰਿਆ ਨਹ ਮਿਲੀਐ ਇਹ ਜੁਗਤਾ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: hau hau karat bandhan meh pariaa neh mileeai ih jugataa |5|
lines.english: He indulges in egotism and pride, and falls into entanglements, but he does not meet the Lord by these devices. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋਗ ਸਿਧ ਆਸਣ ਚਉਰਾਸੀਹ ਏ ਭੀ ਕਰਿ ਕਰਿ ਰਹਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: jog sidh aasan chauraaseeh e bhee kar kar rahiaa |
lines.english: He practices the eighty-four postures of Yoga, and acquires the supernatural powers of the Siddhas, but he gets tired of practicing these.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਡੀ ਆਰਜਾ ਫਿਰਿ ਫਿਰਿ ਜਨਮੈ ਹਰਿ ਸਿਉ ਸੰਗੁ ਨ ਗਹਿਆ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: vaddee aarajaa fir fir janamai har siau sang na gahiaa |6|
lines.english: He lives a long life, but is reincarnated again and again; he has not met with the Lord. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੇਜ ਸੋਹਨੀ ਚੰਦਨੁ ਚੋਆ ਨਰਕ ਘੋਰ ਕਾ ਦੁਆਰਾ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: sej sohanee chandan choaa narak ghor kaa duaaraa |7|
lines.english: He may lie on beautiful beds, perfumed with sandalwood oil, but this will led him only to the gates of the most horrible hell. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਜ ਲੀਲਾ ਰਾਜਨ ਕੀ ਰਚਨਾ ਕਰਿਆ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਅਫਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: raaj leelaa raajan kee rachanaa kariaa hukam afaaraa |
lines.english: He may enjoy princely pleasures, and regal pomp and ceremony, and issue unchallenged commands.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਤਿਸੁ ਭਇਓ ਪਰਾਪਤਿ ਜਿਸੁ ਪੁਰਬ ਲਿਖੇ ਕਾ ਲਹਨਾ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: kahu naanak tis bheo paraapat jis purab likhe kaa lahanaa |8|
lines.english: Says Nanak, he alone obtains it, who is pre-destined to receive it. ||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕੀਰਤਿ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਹੈ ਸਿਰਿ ਕਰਮਨ ਕੈ ਕਰਮਾ ॥
lines.translit: har keerat saadhasangat hai sir karaman kai karamaa |
lines.english: Singing the Kirtan of the Lord's Praises in the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy, is the highest of all actions.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਇਓ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾਲੁ ਦੀਨ ਦੁਖ ਭੰਜਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਕੀਰਤਨਿ ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਰਾਤਾ ॥ ਰਹਾਉ ਦੂਜਾ ॥੧॥੩॥
lines.translit: bheo kripaal deen dukh bhanjan har har keeratan ihu man raataa | rahaau doojaa |1|3|
lines.english: The Destroyer of the pains of the poor has become merciful to me, and this mind is imbued with the Praises of the Lord, Har, Har. ||Second Pause||1||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੇਰੋ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਇਹ ਰੰਗਿ ਮਾਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: tero sevak ih rang maataa |
lines.english: Your slave is intoxicated with this Love of Yours.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਗੁ ਸੋਰਠਿ ਵਾਰ ਮਹਲੇ ੪ ਕੀ ॥
lines.translit: raag soratth vaar mahale 4 kee |
lines.english: Vaar Of Raag Sorat'h, Fourth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੰਦੀ ਮੈਲੁ ਨ ਕਤੁ ਮਨਿ ਜੀਭੈ ਸਚਾ ਸੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: dandee mail na kat man jeebhai sachaa soe |
lines.english: Her teeth are clean and her mind is not split by duality; the Name of the True Lord is on her tongue.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋਰਠਿ ਸਦਾ ਸੁਹਾਵਣੀ ਜੇ ਸਚਾ ਮਨਿ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: soratth sadaa suhaavanee je sachaa man hoe |
lines.english: Sorat'h is always beautiful, if it brings the True Lord to dwell in the mind of the soul-bride.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਹਰਿ ਕਪੜੁ ਜੇ ਪਿਰ ਮਿਲੈ ਖੁਸੀ ਰਾਵੈ ਪਿਰੁ ਸੰਗਿ ॥
lines.translit: parahar kaparr je pir milai khusee raavai pir sang |
lines.english: Discarding worldly adornments, she meets her Husband Lord, and she celebrates joyfully with Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਦਾ ਸੀਗਾਰੀ ਨਾਉ ਮਨਿ ਕਦੇ ਨ ਮੈਲੁ ਪਤੰਗੁ ॥
lines.translit: sadaa seegaaree naau man kade na mail patang |
lines.english: She is adorned forever with the Name in her mind, and she does not have even an iota of filth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਸੁਰੈ ਪੇਈਐ ਭੈ ਵਸੀ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸੇਵਿ ਨਿਸੰਗ ॥
lines.translit: sasurai peeeai bhai vasee satigur sev nisang |
lines.english: Here and hereafter, she abides in the Fear of God, and serves the True Guru without hesitation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਵਰ ਜੇਠ ਮੁਏ ਦੁਖਿ ਸਸੂ ਕਾ ਡਰੁ ਕਿਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: devar jetth mue dukh sasoo kaa ddar kis |
lines.english: Her husband's younger and elder brothers, the corrupt desires, have died, suffering in pain; and now, who fears Maya, the mother-in-law?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਪਿਰ ਭਾਵੈ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਕਰਮ ਮਣੀ ਸਭੁ ਸਚੁ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: je pir bhaavai naanakaa karam manee sabh sach |1|
lines.english: If she becomes pleasing to her Husband Lord, O Nanak, she bears the jewel of good karma upon her forehead, and everything is Truth to her. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋਰਠਿ ਤਾਮਿ ਸੁਹਾਵਣੀ ਜਾ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਢੰਢੋਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: soratth taam suhaavanee jaa har naam dtandtole |
lines.english: Sorat'h is beautiful only when it leads the soul-bride to seek the Lord's Name.

lines.translit: mahalaa 4 |
lines.english: Fourth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਮਨਾਵੈ ਆਪਣਾ ਗੁਰਮਤੀ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਬੋਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: gur purakh manaavai aapanaa guramatee har har bole |
lines.english: She pleases her Guru and God; under Guru's Instruction, she speaks the Name of the Lord, Har, Har.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮਿ ਕਸਾਈ ਦਿਨਸੁ ਰਾਤਿ ਹਰਿ ਰਤੀ ਹਰਿ ਰੰਗਿ ਚੋਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: har prem kasaaee dinas raat har ratee har rang chole |
lines.english: She is attracted to the Lord's Name, day and night, and her body is drenched in the color of the Love of the Lord, Har, Har.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਜੈਸਾ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਨ ਲਭਈ ਸਭੁ ਦੇਖਿਆ ਜਗਤੁ ਮੈ ਟੋਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: har jaisaa purakh na labhee sabh dekhiaa jagat mai ttole |
lines.english: No other being like the Lord God can be found; I have looked and searched over the whole world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਿ ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਦ੍ਰਿੜਾਇਆ ਮਨੁ ਅਨਤ ਨ ਕਾਹੂ ਡੋਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: gur satigur naam drirraaeaa man anat na kaahoo ddole |
lines.english: The Guru, the True Guru, has implanted the Naam within me; my mind does not waver any more.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂ ਆਪੇ ਸਿਸਟਿ ਕਰਤਾ ਸਿਰਜਣਹਾਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: too aape sisatt karataa sirajanahaariaa |
lines.english: You Yourself are the Creator, the Fashioner of the world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨੁ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਦਾਸੁ ਹੈ ਗੁਰ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੇ ਗੋਲ ਗੋਲੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jan naanak har kaa daas hai gur satigur ke gol gole |2|
lines.english: Servant Nanak is the Lord's slave, the slave of the slaves of the Guru, the True Guru. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਾਤਾ ਕਰਤਾ ਆਪਿ ਆਪਿ ਭੋਗਣਹਾਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: daataa karataa aap aap bhoganahaariaa |
lines.english: You Yourself are the Giver and the Creator; You Yourself are the Enjoyer.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਧੁ ਆਪੇ ਖੇਲੁ ਰਚਾਇ ਤੁਧੁ ਆਪਿ ਸਵਾਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: tudh aape khel rachaae tudh aap savaariaa |
lines.english: You Yourself have arranged the play, and You Yourself arrange it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੁ ਤੇਰਾ ਸਬਦੁ ਵਰਤੈ ਉਪਾਵਣਹਾਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: sabh teraa sabad varatai upaavanahaariaa |
lines.english: The Word of Your Shabad is pervading everywhere, O Creator Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਦਾ ਸਲਾਹੀ ਗੁਰ ਕਉ ਵਾਰਿਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: hau guramukh sadaa salaahee gur kau vaariaa |1|
lines.english: As Gurmukh, I ever praise the Lord; I am a sacrifice to the Guru. ||1||
